data.policy_text: The CSIRT shall: Develop, implement, and maintain an incident response plan that includes the following, at a minimum: A roadmap and high-level approach for implementing organization’s incident response capability. A process to define reportable incidents and metrics for measuring the severity and Impact of incidents. Define the required resources and management support to effectively maintain and mature an incident response capability. Documentation that is reviewed and approved by defined personnel or roles. Distribute copies of the incident response plan to identified stakeholders. Update incident response plan regularly based on lessons learned. Communicate incident response plan changes to defined incident response personnel. Protect the incident response plan from unauthorised disclosure and modification. Perform investigations based on information distributed by an information sharing community to reduce the risks of similar incidents. Perform post-incident analysis to identify

data.policy_text: All Employees and 'Third Parties' are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. In addition, violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the 's Human Capital Department, considering the recommendation of IS Team and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws, and regulations. Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record. Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the 'Policy Exception Management procedure.'

data.policy_text: Monitoring performed by third parties shall involve Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDA) and Service Level Agreements (SLA). The SLA shall include procedures for log reporting and escalation. Logs generated and stored shall be monitored in real time by a central log monitoring system. The central log monitoring system shall be capable of centralization, sorting and parsing of the logs collected from different IT Systems to identify, contain and respond quickly to events/incidents in the network A dedicated monitoring team operating under IT Section shall be assigned to centrally monitor the logs generated by all the IT systems. Logs shall not be analyzed by the same individual whose activities are being logged and monitored, to ensure segregation of duties. Corrective and preventive measures on reported incidents shall be taken. Logs shall be backed up on a regular basis, in accordance with the log storage and retention requirements. data.policy_text: Logging requirements shall be determined based on scope, information to be captured in audit logs, frequency for review of audit log data, and applicable laws and regulations. High level log activities that the company would like to track shall be defined. Potential log sources shall be identified for log monitoring based upon a risk assessment and their importance to the business. The critical log sources shall be configured and documented for log generation. Logs generated for activities that affect the availability and security of the systems, shall be configured for monitoring.

data.policy_text: The purpose of this Information Security Incident Management Policy (“Policy”) is to ensure that information security incidents within the organization are communicated in a manner which allows timely corrective actions to be taken. Furthermore, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO27001 and National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF). The following are the key objectives of this Policy: Launch an incident response plan: Development, implementation, and maintenance of a plan to guide incident response activities. Establish a CSIRT: Establishment of a Computer Security Incident Response Team (CSIRT) in charge of the incident management and response plan. Enable reporting: Reporting information security incidents that results in damage or risk to information security assets and operations through appropriate management channels to st

Logs generated for activities that affect the availability and security of the systems, shall be configured for monitoring.

The SLA shall include procedures for log reporting and escalation. Logs generated and stored shall be monitored in real time by a central log monitoring system. The central log monitoring system shall be capable of centralization, sorting and parsing of the logs collected from different IT Systems to identify, contain and respond quickly to events/incidents in the network A dedicated monitoring team operating under IT Section shall be assigned to centrally monitor the logs generated by all the IT systems. Logs shall not be analyzed by the same individual whose activities are being logged and monitored, to ensure segregation of duties. data.policy_text: The purpose of this Information Security Incident Management Policy (“Policy”) is to ensure that information security incidents within the organization are communicated in a manner which allows timely corrective actions to be taken. Furthermore, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO27001 and National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF). The following are the key objectives of this Policy: Launch an incident response plan: Development, implementation, and maintenance of a plan to guide incident response activities. Furthermore, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO27001 and National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF). The following are the key objectives of this Policy: Launch an incident response plan: Development, implementation, and maintenance of a plan to guide incident response activities. Establish a CSIRT: Establishment of a Computer Security Incident Response Team (CSIRT) in charge of the incident management and response plan. Enable reporting: Reporting information security incidents that results in damage or risk to information security assets and operations through appropriate management channels to st

data.policy_text: Monitoring performed by third parties shall involve Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDA) and Service Level Agreements (SLA). The SLA shall include procedures for log reporting and escalation. Logs generated and stored shall be monitored in real time by a central log monitoring system. The central log monitoring system shall be capable of centralization, sorting and parsing of the logs collected from different IT Systems to identify, contain and respond quickly to events/incidents in the network A dedicated monitoring team operating under IT Section shall be assigned to centrally monitor the logs generated by all the IT systems. Logs shall not be analyzed by the same individual whose activities are being logged and monitored, to ensure segregation of duties. Corrective and preventive measures on reported incidents shall be taken. Logs shall be backed up on a regular basis, in accordance with the log storage and retention requirements. data.policy_text: The CSIRT shall: Develop, implement, and maintain an incident response plan that includes the following, at a minimum: A roadmap and high-level approach for implementing organization’s incident response capability. A process to define reportable incidents and metrics for measuring the severity and Impact of incidents. Define the required resources and management support to effectively maintain and mature an incident response capability. Documentation that is reviewed and approved by defined personnel or roles. Distribute copies of the incident response plan to identified stakeholders. Update incident response plan regularly based on lessons learned. Communicate incident response plan changes to defined incident response personnel. Protect the incident response plan from unauthorised disclosure and modification. Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the Policy Exception Management procedure.

data.policy_text: Logging requirements shall be determined based on scope, information to be captured in audit logs, frequency for review of audit log data, and applicable laws and regulations. High level log activities that the company would like to track shall be defined. Potential log sources shall be identified for log monitoring based upon a risk assessment and their importance to the business. The critical log sources shall be configured and documented for log generation. Logs generated for activities that affect the availability and security of the systems, shall be configured for monitoring.

High level log activities that the company would like to track shall be defined. Potential log sources shall be identified for log monitoring based upon a risk assessment and their importance to the business. Logs generated for activities that affect the availability and security of the systems, shall be configured for monitoring.

data.policy_text: Monitoring performed by third parties shall involve Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDA) and Service Level Agreements (SLA). The SLA shall include procedures for log reporting and escalation. Logs generated and stored shall be monitored in real time by a central log monitoring system. The central log monitoring system shall be capable of centralization, sorting and parsing of the logs collected from different IT Systems to identify, contain and respond quickly to events/incidents in the network A dedicated monitoring team operating under IT Section shall be assigned to centrally monitor the logs generated by all the IT systems. Logs shall not be analyzed by the same individual whose activities are being logged and monitored, to ensure segregation of duties. Corrective and preventive measures on reported incidents shall be taken. Logs shall be backed up on a regular basis, in accordance with the log storage and retention requirements. data.policy_text: The purpose of this Information Security Incident Management Policy (“Policy”) is to ensure that information security incidents within the organization are communicated in a manner which allows timely corrective actions to be taken. Furthermore, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO27001 and National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF). The following are the key objectives of this Policy: Launch an incident response plan: Development, implementation, and maintenance of a plan to guide incident response activities. data.policy_text:
This Policy is to be part of all employees, contractors and third parties’ processes, and all are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <CLIENT>'s Human Capital Department, taking into consideration the recommendation of the Information Security department and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations. Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.

Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the Policy Exception Management procedure.

data.control_name: Awareness and Training Policy NONE AT-1 NONE NONE
data.control_text: The entity shall develop and maintain an awareness and training policy.
data.policy_text: The CSIRT shall: Develop, implement, and maintain an incident response plan that includes the following, at a minimum: A roadmap and high-level approach for implementing organization’s incident response capability. A process to define reportable incidents and metrics for measuring the severity and Impact of incidents. Define the required resources and management support to effectively maintain and mature an incident response capability. Documentation that is reviewed and approved by defined personnel or roles. Distribute copies of the incident response plan to identified stakeholders. Update incident response plan regularly based on lessons learned. Communicate incident response plan changes to defined incident response personnel. Protect the incident response plan from unauthorised disclosure and modification. Perform investigations based on information distributed by an information sharing community to reduce the risks of similar incidents. Perform post-incident analysis to identify

data.control_text: The entity shall develop and maintain an awareness and training policy.
data.policy_text: Monitoring performed by third parties shall involve Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDA) and Service Level Agreements (SLA). The SLA shall include procedures for log reporting and escalation. Logs generated and stored shall be monitored in real time by a central log monitoring system. The central log monitoring system shall be capable of centralization, sorting and parsing of the logs collected from different IT Systems to identify, contain and respond quickly to events/incidents in the network A dedicated monitoring team operating under IT Section shall be assigned to centrally monitor the logs generated by all the IT systems. Logs shall not be analyzed by the same individual whose activities are being logged and monitored, to ensure segregation of duties. Corrective and preventive measures on reported incidents shall be taken. Logs shall be backed up on a regular basis, in accordance with the log storage and retention requirements. data.control_name: Awareness and Training Policy NONE AT-1 NONE NONE
data.control_text: The entity shall develop and maintain an awareness and training policy.
data.policy_text: Logging requirements shall be determined based on scope, information to be captured in audit logs, frequency for review of audit log data, and applicable laws and regulations. High level log activities that the company would like to track shall be defined. Potential log sources shall be identified for log monitoring based upon a risk assessment and their importance to the business. The critical log sources shall be configured and documented for log generation. Logs generated for activities that affect the availability and security of the systems, shall be configured for monitoring.

data.policy_text: The purpose of this Information Security Incident Management Policy (“Policy”) is to ensure that information security incidents within the organization are communicated in a manner which allows timely corrective actions to be taken. Furthermore, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO27001 and National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF). The following are the key objectives of this Policy: Launch an incident response plan: Development, implementation, and maintenance of a plan to guide incident response activities. Establish a CSIRT: Establishment of a Computer Security Incident Response Team (CSIRT) in charge of the incident management and response plan. Enable reporting: Reporting information security incidents that results in damage or risk to information security assets and operations through appropriate management channels to st

data.policy_text: The purpose of this Information Security Incident Management Policy (“Policy”) is to ensure that information security incidents within the organization are communicated in a manner which allows timely corrective actions to be taken. Furthermore, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO27001 and National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF). The following are the key objectives of this Policy: Launch an incident response plan: Development, implementation, and maintenance of a plan to guide incident response activities. Establish a CSIRT: Establishment of a Computer Security Incident Response Team (CSIRT) in charge of the incident management and response plan. data.policy_text: The CSIRT shall: Develop, implement, and maintain an incident response plan that includes the following, at a minimum: A roadmap and high-level approach for implementing organization’s incident response capability. A process to define reportable incidents and metrics for measuring the severity and Impact of incidents. Define the required resources and management support to effectively maintain and mature an incident response capability. Documentation that is reviewed and approved by defined personnel or roles. Distribute copies of the incident response plan to identified stakeholders. Update incident response plan regularly based on lessons learned. Communicate incident response plan changes to defined incident response personnel. Protect the incident response plan from unauthorised disclosure and modification. data.policy_text: Logging requirements shall be determined based on scope, information to be captured in audit logs, frequency for review of audit log data, and applicable laws and regulations. High level log activities that the company would like to track shall be defined. Potential log sources shall be identified for log monitoring based upon a risk assessment and their importance to the business. The critical log sources shall be configured and documented for log generation. Logs generated for activities that affect the availability and security of the systems, shall be configured for monitoring.

data.control_text: The entity shall develop and maintain a human resources security policy and associated security controls.
data.policy_text: All Employees and 'Third Parties' are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. In addition, violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the 's Human Capital Department, considering the recommendation of IS Team and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws, and regulations. Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record. Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the 'Policy Exception Management procedure.'

Furthermore, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO27001 and National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF). data.policy_text: All Employees and 'Third Parties' are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. In addition, violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the 's Human Capital Department, considering the recommendation of IS Team and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws, and regulations. Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record. Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the 'Policy Exception Management procedure.'

data.policy_text: Monitoring performed by third parties shall involve Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDA) and Service Level Agreements (SLA). The SLA shall include procedures for log reporting and escalation. Logs generated and stored shall be monitored in real time by a central log monitoring system. The central log monitoring system shall be capable of centralization, sorting and parsing of the logs collected from different IT Systems to identify, contain and respond quickly to events/incidents in the network A dedicated monitoring team operating under IT Section shall be assigned to centrally monitor the logs generated by all the IT systems. Logs shall not be analyzed by the same individual whose activities are being logged and monitored, to ensure segregation of duties. Corrective and preventive measures on reported incidents shall be taken. Logs shall be backed up on a regular basis, in accordance with the log storage and retention requirements. data.policy_text: The purpose of this Information Security Incident Management Policy (“Policy”) is to ensure that information security incidents within the organization are communicated in a manner which allows timely corrective actions to be taken. Furthermore, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO27001 and National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF). The following are the key objectives of this Policy: Launch an incident response plan: Development, implementation, and maintenance of a plan to guide incident response activities. Establish a CSIRT: Establishment of a Computer Security Incident Response Team (CSIRT) in charge of the incident management and response plan. Enable reporting: Reporting information security incidents that results in damage or risk to information security assets and operations through appropriate management channels to st

High level log activities that the company would like to track shall be defined. Potential log sources shall be identified for log monitoring based upon a risk assessment and their importance to the business. The critical log sources shall be configured and documented for log generation. Logs generated for activities that affect the availability and security of the systems, shall be configured for monitoring.

data.policy_text: The CSIRT shall: Develop, implement, and maintain an incident response plan that includes the following, at a minimum: A roadmap and high-level approach for implementing organization’s incident response capability. A process to define reportable incidents and metrics for measuring the severity and Impact of incidents. Define the required resources and management support to effectively maintain and mature an incident response capability. Documentation that is reviewed and approved by defined personnel or roles. Distribute copies of the incident response plan to identified stakeholders. Update incident response plan regularly based on lessons learned. Communicate incident response plan changes to defined incident response personnel. Protect the incident response plan from unauthorised disclosure and modification. Perform investigations based on information distributed by an information sharing community to reduce the risks of similar incidents. Perform post-incident analysis to identify

data.policy_text: Monitoring performed by third parties shall involve Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDA) and Service Level Agreements (SLA). The SLA shall include procedures for log reporting and escalation. Logs generated and stored shall be monitored in real time by a central log monitoring system. The central log monitoring system shall be capable of centralization, sorting and parsing of the logs collected from different IT Systems to identify, contain and respond quickly to events/incidents in the network A dedicated monitoring team operating under IT Section shall be assigned to centrally monitor the logs generated by all the IT systems. Logs shall not be analyzed by the same individual whose activities are being logged and monitored, to ensure segregation of duties. Corrective and preventive measures on reported incidents shall be taken. Logs shall be backed up on a regular basis, in accordance with the log storage and retention requirements. data.policy_text:
This Policy is to be part of all employees, contractors and third parties’ processes, and all are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <CLIENT>'s Human Capital Department, taking into consideration the recommendation of the Information Security department and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations. Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.

Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the Policy Exception Management procedure.

data.policy_text: The purpose of this Information Security Incident Management Policy (“Policy”) is to ensure that information security incidents within the organization are communicated in a manner which allows timely corrective actions to be taken. Furthermore, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO27001 and National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF). The following are the key objectives of this Policy: Launch an incident response plan: Development, implementation, and maintenance of a plan to guide incident response activities. data.policy_text: Logging requirements shall be determined based on scope, information to be captured in audit logs, frequency for review of audit log data, and applicable laws and regulations. High level log activities that the company would like to track shall be defined. Potential log sources shall be identified for log monitoring based upon a risk assessment and their importance to the business. The critical log sources shall be configured and documented for log generation. Logs generated for activities that affect the availability and security of the systems, shall be configured for monitoring.

data.policy_text: Monitoring performed by third parties shall involve Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDA) and Service Level Agreements (SLA). The SLA shall include procedures for log reporting and escalation. Logs generated and stored shall be monitored in real time by a central log monitoring system. The central log monitoring system shall be capable of centralization, sorting and parsing of the logs collected from different IT Systems to identify, contain and respond quickly to events/incidents in the network A dedicated monitoring team operating under IT Section shall be assigned to centrally monitor the logs generated by all the IT systems. Logs shall not be analyzed by the same individual whose activities are being logged and monitored, to ensure segregation of duties. Corrective and preventive measures on reported incidents shall be taken. Logs shall be backed up on a regular basis, in accordance with the log storage and retention requirements. data.policy_text: The purpose of this Information Security Incident Management Policy (“Policy”) is to ensure that information security incidents within the organization are communicated in a manner which allows timely corrective actions to be taken. Furthermore, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO27001 and National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF). The following are the key objectives of this Policy: Launch an incident response plan: Development, implementation, and maintenance of a plan to guide incident response activities. Establish a CSIRT: Establishment of a Computer Security Incident Response Team (CSIRT) in charge of the incident management and response plan. Furthermore, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO27001 and National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF). data.policy_text: Monitoring performed by third parties shall involve Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDA) and Service Level Agreements (SLA). The SLA shall include procedures for log reporting and escalation. Logs generated and stored shall be monitored in real time by a central log monitoring system. The central log monitoring system shall be capable of centralization, sorting and parsing of the logs collected from different IT Systems to identify, contain and respond quickly to events/incidents in the network A dedicated monitoring team operating under IT Section shall be assigned to centrally monitor the logs generated by all the IT systems. Logs shall not be analyzed by the same individual whose activities are being logged and monitored, to ensure segregation of duties. Corrective and preventive measures on reported incidents shall be taken. Logs shall be backed up on a regular basis, in accordance with the log storage and retention requirements. data.policy_text: The purpose of this Information Security Incident Management Policy (“Policy”) is to ensure that information security incidents within the organization are communicated in a manner which allows timely corrective actions to be taken. Furthermore, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO27001 and National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF). The following are the key objectives of this Policy: Launch an incident response plan: Development, implementation, and maintenance of a plan to guide incident response activities. data.policy_text: Monitoring performed by third parties shall involve Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDA) and Service Level Agreements (SLA). The SLA shall include procedures for log reporting and escalation. Logs generated and stored shall be monitored in real time by a central log monitoring system. The central log monitoring system shall be capable of centralization, sorting and parsing of the logs collected from different IT Systems to identify, contain and respond quickly to events/incidents in the network A dedicated monitoring team operating under IT Section shall be assigned to centrally monitor the logs generated by all the IT systems. Logs shall not be analyzed by the same individual whose activities are being logged and monitored, to ensure segregation of duties. data.control_text: The entity shall document operating procedures.
data.policy_text: The purpose of this Information Security Incident Management Policy (“Policy”) is to ensure that information security incidents within the organization are communicated in a manner which allows timely corrective actions to be taken. Furthermore, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO27001 and National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF). The following are the key objectives of this Policy: Launch an incident response plan: Development, implementation, and maintenance of a plan to guide incident response activities. Establish a CSIRT: Establishment of a Computer Security Incident Response Team (CSIRT) in charge of the incident management and response plan. Enable reporting: Reporting information security incidents that results in damage or risk to information security assets and operations through appropriate management channels to st

data.policy_text: Monitoring performed by third parties shall involve Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDA) and Service Level Agreements (SLA). The SLA shall include procedures for log reporting and escalation. Logs generated and stored shall be monitored in real time by a central log monitoring system. The central log monitoring system shall be capable of centralization, sorting and parsing of the logs collected from different IT Systems to identify, contain and respond quickly to events/incidents in the network A dedicated monitoring team operating under IT Section shall be assigned to centrally monitor the logs generated by all the IT systems. Logs shall not be analyzed by the same individual whose activities are being logged and monitored, to ensure segregation of duties. Corrective and preventive measures on reported incidents shall be taken. Logs shall be backed up on a regular basis, in accordance with the log storage and retention requirements. data.control_text: logs when a user attempts to access resources without the appropriate privileges. To evaluate whether such logging is in place, an entity should periodically scan through its logs and compare them with the asset inventory in order to ensure that each managed item actively connected to the network is periodically generating logs. Analytical programs such as SIM/SEM solutions for reviewing logs can provide value, but the capabilities employed to analyze audit logs are quite extensive, including, importantly, even just a cursory examination by a person. Actual correlation tools can make audit logs far more useful for subsequent manual inspection. Such tools can be quite helpful in identifying subtle attacks. However, these tools are neither a panacea nor a replacement for skilled information security personnel and system administrators. Even with automated log analysis tools, human expertise and intuition are often required to identify and understand attacks. Relevant Threats and • Unauthorized access Vulnerabilities • Tempering with information systems • Backdoor or command and control
data.policy_text: Monitoring performed by third parties shall involve Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDA) and Service Level Agreements (SLA). The SLA shall include procedures for log reporting and escalation. Logs generated and stored shall be monitored in real time by a central log monitoring system. The central log monitoring system shall be capable of centralization, sorting and parsing of the logs collected from different IT Systems to identify, contain and respond quickly to events/incidents in the network A dedicated monitoring team operating under IT Section shall be assigned to centrally monitor the logs generated by all the IT systems. Logs shall not be analyzed by the same individual whose activities are being logged and monitored, to ensure segregation of duties. Corrective and preventive measures on reported incidents shall be taken. Logs shall be backed up on a regular basis, in accordance with the log storage and retention requirements. data.policy_text: The purpose of this Information Security Incident Management Policy (“Policy”) is to ensure that information security incidents within the organization are communicated in a manner which allows timely corrective actions to be taken. Furthermore, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO27001 and National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF). The following are the key objectives of this Policy: Launch an incident response plan: Development, implementation, and maintenance of a plan to guide incident response activities. Establish a CSIRT: Establishment of a Computer Security Incident Response Team (CSIRT) in charge of the incident management and response plan. Enable reporting: Reporting information security incidents that results in damage or risk to information security assets and operations through appropriate management channels to st

data.policy_text: The purpose of this Information Security Incident Management Policy (“Policy”) is to ensure that information security incidents within the organization are communicated in a manner which allows timely corrective actions to be taken. Furthermore, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO27001 and National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF). The following are the key objectives of this Policy: Launch an incident response plan: Development, implementation, and maintenance of a plan to guide incident response activities. Establish a CSIRT: Establishment of a Computer Security Incident Response Team (CSIRT) in charge of the incident management and response plan. Logs generated for activities that affect the availability and security of the systems, shall be configured for monitoring.

data.policy_text: Monitoring performed by third parties shall involve Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDA) and Service Level Agreements (SLA). The SLA shall include procedures for log reporting and escalation. Logs generated and stored shall be monitored in real time by a central log monitoring system. The central log monitoring system shall be capable of centralization, sorting and parsing of the logs collected from different IT Systems to identify, contain and respond quickly to events/incidents in the network A dedicated monitoring team operating under IT Section shall be assigned to centrally monitor the logs generated by all the IT systems. Logs shall not be analyzed by the same individual whose activities are being logged and monitored, to ensure segregation of duties. data.policy_text: The purpose of this Information Security Incident Management Policy (“Policy”) is to ensure that information security incidents within the organization are communicated in a manner which allows timely corrective actions to be taken. Furthermore, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO27001 and National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF). The following are the key objectives of this Policy: Launch an incident response plan: Development, implementation, and maintenance of a plan to guide incident response activities. This Policy is to be part of all employees, contractors and third parties’ processes, and all are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.

Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the Policy Exception Management procedure.

data.policy_text: The purpose of this Information Security Incident Management Policy (“Policy”) is to ensure that information security incidents within the organization are communicated in a manner which allows timely corrective actions to be taken. Furthermore, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO27001 and National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF). The following are the key objectives of this Policy: Launch an incident response plan: Development, implementation, and maintenance of a plan to guide incident response activities. data.control_name: Information Systems Acquisition, Development and Maintenance Policy NONE SA-1, MA-1, SI-1 NONE NONE
data.control_text: The entity shall establish an information systems acquisition, development, and maintenance policy.
data.policy_text: Monitoring performed by third parties shall involve Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDA) and Service Level Agreements (SLA). The SLA shall include procedures for log reporting and escalation. Logs generated and stored shall be monitored in real time by a central log monitoring system. The central log monitoring system shall be capable of centralization, sorting and parsing of the logs collected from different IT Systems to identify, contain and respond quickly to events/incidents in the network A dedicated monitoring team operating under IT Section shall be assigned to centrally monitor the logs generated by all the IT systems. Logs shall not be analyzed by the same individual whose activities are being logged and monitored, to ensure segregation of duties. Corrective and preventive measures on reported incidents shall be taken. Logs shall be backed up on a regular basis, in accordance with the log storage and retention requirements. data.policy_text: The CSIRT shall: Develop, implement, and maintain an incident response plan that includes the following, at a minimum: A roadmap and high-level approach for implementing organization’s incident response capability. A process to define reportable incidents and metrics for measuring the severity and Impact of incidents. Define the required resources and management support to effectively maintain and mature an incident response capability. Documentation that is reviewed and approved by defined personnel or roles. Distribute copies of the incident response plan to identified stakeholders. Update incident response plan regularly based on lessons learned. Communicate incident response plan changes to defined incident response personnel. Protect the incident response plan from unauthorised disclosure and modification. data.policy_text:
This Policy is to be part of all employees, contractors and third parties’ processes, and all are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <CLIENT>'s Human Capital Department, taking into consideration the recommendation of the Information Security department and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations. Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.

Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the Policy Exception Management procedure.

data.policy_text: Logging requirements shall be determined based on scope, information to be captured in audit logs, frequency for review of audit log data, and applicable laws and regulations. High level log activities that the company would like to track shall be defined. Potential log sources shall be identified for log monitoring based upon a risk assessment and their importance to the business. The critical log sources shall be configured and documented for log generation. Logs generated for activities that affect the availability and security of the systems, shall be configured for monitoring.

data.policy_text: The purpose of this Information Security Incident Management Policy (“Policy”) is to ensure that information security incidents within the organization are communicated in a manner which allows timely corrective actions to be taken. Furthermore, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO27001 and National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF). The following are the key objectives of this Policy: Launch an incident response plan: Development, implementation, and maintenance of a plan to guide incident response activities. Establish a CSIRT: Establishment of a Computer Security Incident Response Team (CSIRT) in charge of the incident management and response plan. Enable reporting: Reporting information security incidents that results in damage or risk to information security assets and operations through appropriate management channels to st

Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <CLIENT>'s Human Capital Department, taking into consideration the recommendation of the Information Security department and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations. Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.

Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the Policy Exception Management procedure.

data.policy_text: The CSIRT shall: Develop, implement, and maintain an incident response plan that includes the following, at a minimum: A roadmap and high-level approach for implementing organization’s incident response capability. A process to define reportable incidents and metrics for measuring the severity and Impact of incidents. Define the required resources and management support to effectively maintain and mature an incident response capability. Documentation that is reviewed and approved by defined personnel or roles. Distribute copies of the incident response plan to identified stakeholders. Update incident response plan regularly based on lessons learned. Communicate incident response plan changes to defined incident response personnel. Protect the incident response plan from unauthorised disclosure and modification. Perform investigations based on information distributed by an information sharing community to reduce the risks of similar incidents. Perform post-incident analysis to identify

data.policy_text: Monitoring performed by third parties shall involve Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDA) and Service Level Agreements (SLA). The SLA shall include procedures for log reporting and escalation. Logs generated and stored shall be monitored in real time by a central log monitoring system. The central log monitoring system shall be capable of centralization, sorting and parsing of the logs collected from different IT Systems to identify, contain and respond quickly to events/incidents in the network A dedicated monitoring team operating under IT Section shall be assigned to centrally monitor the logs generated by all the IT systems. Logs shall not be analyzed by the same individual whose activities are being logged and monitored, to ensure segregation of duties. Corrective and preventive measures on reported incidents shall be taken. Logs shall be backed up on a regular basis, in accordance with the log storage and retention requirements. data.policy_text: Logging requirements shall be determined based on scope, information to be captured in audit logs, frequency for review of audit log data, and applicable laws and regulations. High level log activities that the company would like to track shall be defined. Potential log sources shall be identified for log monitoring based upon a risk assessment and their importance to the business. The critical log sources shall be configured and documented for log generation. Logs generated for activities that affect the availability and security of the systems, shall be configured for monitoring.

data.policy_text: The purpose of this Information Security Incident Management Policy (“Policy”) is to ensure that information security incidents within the organization are communicated in a manner which allows timely corrective actions to be taken. Furthermore, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO27001 and National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF). The following are the key objectives of this Policy: Launch an incident response plan: Development, implementation, and maintenance of a plan to guide incident response activities. Establish a CSIRT: Establishment of a Computer Security Incident Response Team (CSIRT) in charge of the incident management and response plan. data.policy_text: Monitoring performed by third parties shall involve Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDA) and Service Level Agreements (SLA). The SLA shall include procedures for log reporting and escalation. Logs generated and stored shall be monitored in real time by a central log monitoring system. The central log monitoring system shall be capable of centralization, sorting and parsing of the logs collected from different IT Systems to identify, contain and respond quickly to events/incidents in the network A dedicated monitoring team operating under IT Section shall be assigned to centrally monitor the logs generated by all the IT systems. Logs shall not be analyzed by the same individual whose activities are being logged and monitored, to ensure segregation of duties. Corrective and preventive measures on reported incidents shall be taken. Logs shall be backed up on a regular basis, in accordance with the log storage and retention requirements. data.policy_text: The purpose of this Information Security Incident Management Policy (“Policy”) is to ensure that information security incidents within the organization are communicated in a manner which allows timely corrective actions to be taken. Furthermore, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO27001 and National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF). The following are the key objectives of this Policy: Launch an incident response plan: Development, implementation, and maintenance of a plan to guide incident response activities. Establish a CSIRT: Establishment of a Computer Security Incident Response Team (CSIRT) in charge of the incident management and response plan. High level log activities that the company would like to track shall be defined. Potential log sources shall be identified for log monitoring based upon a risk assessment and their importance to the business. Logs generated for activities that affect the availability and security of the systems, shall be configured for monitoring.

data.policy_text: The purpose of this Information Security Incident Management Policy (“Policy”) is to ensure that information security incidents within the organization are communicated in a manner which allows timely corrective actions to be taken. Furthermore, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO27001 and National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF). The following are the key objectives of this Policy: Launch an incident response plan: Development, implementation, and maintenance of a plan to guide incident response activities. The reporting shall capture detail such as top 10 sources/targets, source countries, failed logins etc. Frequency of log reporting shall be determined based on the criticality of systems and audience.

data.control_text: The entity shall test, maintain and re-assess its information systems continuity plans.
data.policy_text: Logs shall capture the below details - at minimum: User IDs. Date and Time of the event. Details of the event. Details of the Asset and its location where possible. For applications and  following log types shall be monitored - at minimum: Security Logs. Administrative and Operator Logs. Fault Logs. Backup logs.

data.policy_text:
This Policy is to be part of all employees, contractors and third parties’ processes, and all are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <CLIENT>'s Human Capital Department, taking into consideration the recommendation of the Information Security department and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations. Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.

Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the Policy Exception Management procedure.
